subject: modebar button
new: sendChartToCloud
truth: Stop using 'sendDataToCloud' modebar button; use 'sendChartToCloud' instead.
quote: Rename `sendDataToCloud` modebar button to `sendChartToCloud`, and update to upload chart to Plotly Cloud

subject: __eq__ method on graph_objects classes
old: returns True/False when comparing graph_objects instances
new: returns NotImplemented to give the other operand an opportunity to handle the comparison
truth: The __eq__ method on graph_objects classes now returns NotImplemented instead of True/False, which changes how equality comparisons behave when comparing with non-plotly objects.
quote: The `__eq__` method for `graph_objects` classes now returns `NotImplemented` to give the other operand an opportunity to handle the comparison

from_fact: returns true/false when comparing graph_objects instances
returns notimplemented to give the other operand an opportunity to handle the comparison

subject: minimum numpy version
new: requires numpy >= 1.22
truth: numpy with a version less than 1.22 is no longer supported.
quote: numpy with a version less than 1.22 is no longer supported.

subject: create_hexbin_mapbox function
new: create_hexbin_map
truth: Stop using create_hexbin_mapbox; use create_hexbin_map instead.
quote: Deprecate create_hexbin_mapbox in favor of create_hexbin_map, update related function calls

subject: axis autorange behaviour with bar chart outside text labels
old: axis autorange may clip outside text labels on bars
new: axis autorange increases to avoid clipping outside text labels on bars
truth: Axis autorange now increases when bar charts have outside text labels to prevent clipping, which may change the axis range of existing plots.
quote: Increase axis autorange when bar charts have outside text labels, to avoid labels being clipped

from_fact: axis autorange may clip outside text labels on bars
axis autorange increases to avoid clipping outside text labels on bars

subject: image export engine
new: Kaleido >= v1.0.0
truth: Kaleido v0 is deprecated and will emit deprecation warnings; use Kaleido >= v1.0.0 for image export.
quote: Add deprecation warnings when using Kaleido v0 or deprecated image export features

subject: fig.show() behaviour with ipython
old: fig.show() may have worked incorrectly when ipython is installed
new: fig.show() now behaves correctly when ipython is installed
truth: fig.show() behaviour when ipython is installed has been fixed; existing code relying on the broken behaviour may break.
quote: Fix incorrect fig.show() behavior when ipython is installed

from_fact: fig.show() may have worked incorrectly when ipython is installed
fig.show() now behaves correctly when ipython is installed

subject: width/height in layout during Kaleido export
old: width/height in plot layout were ignored during Kaleido image export
new: width/height in plot layout are now respected during Kaleido image export
truth: Width and height in plot layout are now respected during Kaleido image export; existing code that relied on them being ignored may produce different-sized images.
quote: Fix issue where width/height in plot layout were not respected during Kaleido image export

from_fact: width/height in plot layout were ignored during kaleido image export
width/height in plot layout are now respected during kaleido image export

subject: pointcloud trace
truth: Stop using the pointcloud trace type; it has been removed from the API.
quote: Drop deprecated `pointcloud` and `heatmapgl` traces from the API

subject: heatmapgl trace
truth: Stop using the heatmapgl trace type; it has been removed from the API.
quote: Drop deprecated `pointcloud` and `heatmapgl` traces from the API

subject: tenacity dependency
truth: Stop relying on tenacity being available as a transitive dependency; it has been dropped.
quote: Drop `tenacity` dependency

subject: Jupyter Notebook version 6 and earlier support
truth: Stop using Jupyter Notebook version 6 or earlier; the minimum supported version is now 7.0.0.
quote: Drop support for Jupyter Notebook version 6 and earlier [[#4822](https://github.com/plotly/plotly.py/pull/4822)].

subject: titlefont, titleposition, titleside, titleoffset attributes
truth: Stop using titlefont, titleposition, titleside, and titleoffset attributes; they have been removed.
quote: Drop support for deprecated attributes `titlefont`, `titleposition`, `titleside`, and `titleoffset`

subject: pointcloud and heatmapgl traces and gl2d subplots
old: pointcloud traces, heatmapgl traces, gl2d subplots
truth: Stop using pointcloud and heatmapgl traces and gl2d subplots; they have been removed.
quote: Drop deprecated pointcloud and heatmapgl traces and gl2d subplots

from_fact: pointcloud traces, heatmapgl traces, gl2d subplots

subject: bardir attribute
new: orientation
truth: Stop using bardir; use orientation instead.
quote: Drop support for deprecated `bardir` attribute (use `orientation` instead)

subject: annotation.ref attribute
new: annotation.xref and annotation.yref
truth: Stop using annotation.ref; use annotation.xref and annotation.yref instead.
quote: Drop support for deprecated `annotation.ref` attribute (use `annotation.xref` and `annotation.yref` instead)

subject: error bar opacity attribute
new: alpha channel of error bar color attribute
truth: Stop using error bar opacity attribute; use the alpha channel of the error bar color attribute instead.
quote: Drop support for deprecated error bar `opacity` attribute (use alpha channel of error bar `color` attribute instead)

from_fact: alpha channel of error bar color attribute

subject: gl3d.cameraposition attribute
new: gl3d.camera
truth: Stop using gl3d.cameraposition; use gl3d.camera instead.
quote: Drop support for deprecated attribute `gl3d.cameraposition` (use `gl3d.camera` instead)

subject: plot3dPixelRatio config option
truth: Stop using plot3dPixelRatio in config; it has been removed.
quote: Drop deprecated `plot3dPixelRatio` from config

subject: zauto, zmin, zmax attributes on surface traces
old: zauto, zmin, zmax on surface traces
truth: Stop using zauto, zmin, and zmax on surface traces; they have been removed.
quote: Drop deprecated `zauto`, `zmin` and `zmax` from the surface trace

from_fact: zauto, zmin, zmax on surface traces

subject: autotick attributes on cartesian axes
truth: Stop using autotick attributes on cartesian axes; they have been removed.
quote: Drop deprecated `autotick` attributes from cartesian axes

subject: transforms API
truth: Stop using transforms in the API; they have been removed.
quote: Drop `transforms` from the API

subject: Mapbox-based traces
new: Maplibre-based traces
truth: Mapbox-based traces are deprecated; migrate to Maplibre-based traces.
quote: Deprecate Mapbox-based traces.

subject: Mapbox traces and mapbox subplot
old: mapbox traces (scattermapbox, choroplethmapbox, densitymapbox) and mapbox subplot
new: Use maplibre-based traces (scattermap, choroplethmap, densitymap) and map subplots instead
truth: Stop using mapbox traces (scattermapbox, choroplethmapbox, densitymapbox) and mapbox subplot; use maplibre-based scattermap, choroplethmap, densitymap traces and map subplots instead.
quote: Deprecate mapbox traces and mapbox subplot

from_fact: mapbox traces (scattermapbox, choroplethmapbox, densitymapbox) and mapbox subplot
use maplibre-based traces (scattermap, choroplethmap, densitymap) and map subplots instead

subject: Stamen map tile styles
old: Stamen styles pointing to old endpoint
new: Stamen styles now point to stadiamaps.com
truth: Stamen map tile styles now point to stadiamaps.com and require an API key via config.mapboxAccessToken; existing code using Stamen styles without an API key will break.
quote: Adjust stamen styles to point to `stadiamaps.com`, the users may also need to provide their own API_KEY via `config.mapboxAccessToken`

from_fact: stamen styles pointing to old endpoint
stamen styles now point to stadiamaps.com

subject: Python 3.6 and 3.7 support
truth: Python 3.6 and Python 3.7 are no longer supported; code running on those versions will fail to install or run.
quote: Removed Python 3.6 and Python 3.7 support

subject: autorange options
new: autorange now supports 'min', 'max', 'min reversed', 'max reversed'
truth: Stop using only 'reversed' for autorange; use 'min reversed' or 'max reversed' for partial-range reversed autoranging.
quote: Add "min", "max", "min reversed" and "max reversed" autorange options and handle partial ranges (i.e.

subject: scaleanchor
old: scaleanchor could not be set to false to remove a default constraint
new: scaleanchor now accepts 'false' to remove a constraint set by default
truth: Stop assuming scaleanchor cannot be disabled; set scaleanchor='false' to remove a default axis scaling constraint.
quote: Add "false" option to `scaleanchor` to allow removing a constraint that is set by default [[#6712](https://github.com/plotly/plotly.js/pull/6712)], with thanks to @lvlte for the contribution!

from_fact: scaleanchor could not be set to false to remove a default constraint
scaleanchor now accepts 'false' to remove a constraint set by default

subject: insiderange on cartesian axes
old: insiderange did not exist; grid lines and tick labels of counter axis could overlap when positioned inside
new: insiderange is now available on cartesian axes to avoid overlap
truth: Stop manually positioning tick labels to avoid overlap; use insiderange on cartesian axes to prevent overlap between visible grid lines and tick labels of the counter axis when positioned inside.
quote: Add insiderange to cartesian axes to help avoid overlap between visible grid lines and tick labels of the counter axis when they are positioned inside

from_fact: insiderange did not exist; grid lines and tick labels of counter axis could overlap when positioned inside
insiderange is now available on cartesian axes to avoid overlap

subject: setuptools arg use_2to3
truth: Stop using the `use_2to3` argument in setuptools configuration as it has been removed.
quote: Remove `use_2to3` setuptools arg, which is invalid in the latest Python and setuptools versions

subject: distutils.Version
new: packaging.Version
truth: Stop using distutils.Version and use packaging.Version instead.
quote: Updated distutils.Version to packaging.Version [#3897](https://github.com/plotly/plotly.py/pull/3897)] and [#4055](https://github.com/plotly/plotly.py/pull/4055)]

subject: ff.create_annotated_heatmap
truth: Stop using ff.create_annotated_heatmap as it is deprecated.
quote: Deprecated `ff.create_annotated_heatmap`, `ff.create_county_choropleth`, `ff.create_gantt`

subject: ff.create_county_choropleth
truth: Stop using ff.create_county_choropleth as it is deprecated.
quote: Deprecated `ff.create_annotated_heatmap`, `ff.create_county_choropleth`, `ff.create_gantt`

subject: ff.create_gantt
truth: Stop using ff.create_gantt as it is deprecated.
quote: Deprecated `ff.create_annotated_heatmap`, `ff.create_county_choropleth`, `ff.create_gantt`

subject: JSON serialization key ordering
new: JSON keys are no longer sorted
truth: Stop relying on JSON keys being sorted alphabetically; the serialization engines no longer sort keys.
quote: The JSON serialization engines no longer sort their keys

from_fact: json keys are no longer sorted

subject: pandas/numpy arrays with dtype Object
old: arrays with dtype Object were kept as arrays when added to a Figure
new: arrays with dtype Object are converted to list values when added to a Figure
truth: pandas/numpy arrays with dtype of Object are now converted to list values when added to a Figure (this was a regression in 5.0.0 that was fixed, but the fix changes behaviour from pre-5.0.0)
quote: Fixed regression introduced in version 5.0.0 where pandas/numpy arrays with dtype of Object were being converted to list values when added to a Figure

from_fact: arrays with dtype object were kept as arrays when added to a figure
arrays with dtype object are converted to list values when added to a figure

subject: Python version support
truth: Stop using Python versions older than 3.6; they are no longer supported.
quote: Dropped support for Python older than 3.6

subject: area traces
truth: Stop using `area` traces; they have been removed.
quote: dropped support for long-deprecated `graph_objects` like `area` traces

subject: scatter.(t|r) attributes
truth: Stop using `scatter.t` and `scatter.r` attributes; they have been removed.
quote: Introduce group attributes for `scatter` trace i.e.

subject: layout.(radial|angular)axis attributes
truth: Stop using `layout.radialaxis` and `layout.angularaxis` attributes; they have been removed.
quote: dropped support for long-deprecated `graph_objects` like `area` traces and `scatter.(t|r)` and `layout.(radial|angular)axis` attributes

subject: modebar default buttons
old: modebar had hovermode and spikeline buttons by default
new: modebar no longer has hovermode or spikeline buttons by default
truth: Modebar no longer shows hovermode or spikeline buttons by default; add them back with `fig.update_layout(modebar_add=["v1hovermode", "toggleSpikeLines"])`.
quote: modebar no longer has hovermode or spikeline buttons by default (can be added back with `fig.update_layout(modebar_add=["v1hovermode", "toggleSpikeLines"])`)

from_fact: modebar had hovermode and spikeline buttons by default
modebar no longer has hovermode or spikeline buttons by default

subject: legend item text
old: "Aa" text appeared on legend items by default
new: "Aa" text only appears on legend items when `mode="text"`
truth: The "Aa" text no longer appears on legend items unless `mode="text"` is set.
quote: "Aa" text no longer appears on legend items unless `mode="text"`

from_fact: "aa" text appeared on legend items by default
"aa" text only appears on legend items when `mode="text"`

subject: bar trace textposition default
new: textposition defaults to "auto"
truth: In `bar` traces, `textposition` now defaults to `"auto"` instead of its previous default.
quote: In `bar` traces, `textposition` now defaults to `"auto"`

subject: heatmapgl traces
truth: Stop using `heatmapgl` traces; they are deprecated.
quote: deprecated `heatmapgl`, `pointcloud` traces

subject: pointcloud traces
truth: Stop using `pointcloud` traces; they are deprecated.
quote: deprecated `heatmapgl`, `pointcloud` traces

subject: transform attributes
truth: Stop using all `transform` attributes; they are deprecated.
quote: deprecated `heatmapgl`, `pointcloud` traces as well as all `transform` attributes

subject: retrying dependency
new: tenacity
truth: Stop using the `retrying` dependency; it has been replaced with `tenacity`.
quote: Replaced `retrying` dependency with `tenacity`

subject: Plotly Express category_orders behaviour
old: category_orders values absent from data were ignored for discrete mappings
new: category_orders always takes into account every value, even those absent from data
truth: Plotly Express now always considers every value in `category_orders` for discrete mappings and facets, even values not present in the data.
quote: Plotly Express now always takes into account every value in `category_orders` when computing discrete mappings (color, symbol, line-dash, pattern-shapes) as well as facets, even those values which are absent in the data

from_fact: category_orders values absent from data were ignored for discrete mappings
category_orders always takes into account every value, even those absent from data

subject: axes auto-typing of numeric strings
old: numeric strings were auto-typed as linear
new: numeric strings are auto-typed as categorical data in default templates
truth: Axes now auto-type numeric strings as categorical data instead of linear in default templates, which may break code expecting numeric axis behavior.
quote: axes will now auto-type numeric strings as categorical data rather than linear in the default templates

from_fact: numeric strings were auto-typed as linear
numeric strings are auto-typed as categorical data in default templates

subject: default template autotypenumbers
old: autotypenumbers was not strict in default templates
new: autotypenumbers is set to strict in default templates
truth: The default template now sets autotypenumbers to strict, so numeric strings will no longer be auto-converted to numbers on axes.
quote: a new `autotypenumbers` attribute which is now set to `strict` in the default templates

from_fact: autotypenumbers was not strict in default templates
autotypenumbers is set to strict in default templates

subject: px.imshow x and y parameters for Image trace
old: x and y parameters were ignored when an Image trace was used (RGB data or binary_string=True)
new: x and y parameters are now used for Image traces, but only numerical values are accepted (no date/string values)
truth: px.imshow now uses x and y parameters for Image traces (RGB/binary_string=True), but only accepts numerical values, rejecting date or string values that worked with Heatmap traces.
quote: the `x` and `y` parameters of `px.imshow` are now used also in the case where an Image trace is used (for RGB data or with `binary_string=True`). However, only numerical values are accepted (while the Heatmap trace allows date or string values for `x` and `y`).

from_fact: x and y parameters were ignored when an image trace was used (rgb data or binary_string=true)
x and y parameters are now used for image traces, but only numerical values are accepted (no date/string values)

subject: Plotly Express datetime handling
old: Plotly Express automatically converted datetime columns of input dataframes to UTC
truth: Plotly Express no longer converts datetime columns of input dataframes to UTC, so datetime values will remain in their original timezone.
quote: Plotly Express no longer converts datetime columns of input dataframes to UTC

from_fact: plotly express automatically converted datetime columns of input dataframes to utc

subject: add_traces() rows/cols parameter
new: accepts bare int-like values and lists thereof
truth: add_traces() now accepts int-like values (e.g. numpy ints) for rows/cols, not just Python int.
quote: `add_traces()` now accepts bare `int`-like values for `rows`/`cols` as well as lists thereof

from_fact: accepts bare int-like values and lists thereof

subject: row/col parameter type acceptance
new: accepts int-like values
truth: row and col parameters now accept int-like values (e.g. numpy ints), not strictly Python int.
quote: `row`/`col` now accept `int`-like values, not strictly `int` values

subject: px.bar missing value inference
old: missing x or y defaulted to integers starting at 0 per trace
new: missing value treated as column of 1s named 'count' when provided value is not continuous
truth: In px.bar, when x or y is missing and the provided value is not continuous, the missing axis now defaults to a column of 1s named 'count' instead of per-trace integers starting at 0.
quote: In the case of `px.bar`, if the provided value is not continuous, the missing value is treated as a column of 1s named "count", so as to behave more like `px.histogram` and to avoid sizing the resulting bars differently

from_fact: missing x or y defaulted to integers starting at 0 per trace
missing value treated as column of 1s named 'count' when provided value is not continuous

subject: px.histogram histfunc default
old: histfunc defaulted to something that ignored provided data
new: histfunc now defaults to 'sum' when both x and y are provided (or x,y,z for density_heatmap/density_contour)
truth: In px.histogram (and density_heatmap/density_contour), when both x and y (and z) are provided, histfunc now defaults to 'sum' instead of ignoring the provided data.
quote: if both x and y are provided to histogram, and if x, y and z are provided to density_heatmap or density_contour, then histfunc now defaults to sum so as to avoid ignoring the provided data, and to cause histogram and bar to behave more similarly.

from_fact: histfunc defaulted to something that ignored provided data
histfunc now defaults to 'sum' when both x and y are provided (or x,y,z for density_heatmap/density_contour)

subject: violinmode/boxmode/stripmode default
new: default to 'overlay' when x (or y in h orientation) is also mapped to color
truth: violinmode, boxmode and stripmode now default to 'overlay' instead of 'group' when the categorical axis is also mapped to color.
quote: violinmode, boxmode and stripmode now default to overlay if x (y) in v (h) orientation is also mapped to color, to avoid strange spacing issues with the previous default of group in all cases.

from_fact: default to 'overlay' when x (or y in h orientation) is also mapped to color

subject: missing x and y with data_frame
new: data frame treated as 'wide' data and melted internally
truth: When neither x nor y is provided but data_frame is, Plotly Express now treats the data frame as 'wide' data and melts it internally instead of producing an empty figure.
quote: if neither x nor y is provided but data_frame is, the data frame will be treated as "wide" with defaults depending on the value of orientation (and orientation has accordingly been added to scatter, line, density_heatmap, and density_contour for this purpose). Previously this would have resulted in an empty figure.

from_fact: data frame treated as 'wide' data and melted internally

subject: missing x or y inference
old: defaulted to integers starting at 0 per trace
new: inferred to be the index of data_frame if provided, otherwise a stable index of integers starting at 0
truth: When x or y is missing, it is now inferred as the data_frame index (if data_frame provided) or a stable index starting at 0, instead of per-trace integers starting at 0.
quote: if `x` or `y` is missing, it is inferred to be the index of `data_frame` if `data_frame` provided, otherwise a stable index of integers starting at 0.

from_fact: defaulted to integers starting at 0 per trace
inferred to be the index of data_frame if provided, otherwise a stable index of integers starting at 0

subject: Child graph objects (e.g. figure.layout.xaxis)
old: created eagerly during graph object construction
new: created lazily the first time the property is accessed
truth: Child graph objects like figure.layout.xaxis are no longer created eagerly during construction; they are created lazily on first property access.
quote: Child graph objects (e.g. figure.layout.xaxis) are no longer created eagerly during graph object construction. Instead, they are created lazily the first time the property is accessed.

from_fact: created eagerly during graph object construction
created lazily the first time the property is accessed

subject: px.defaults
new: no longer accepts arbitrary keys
truth: Stop setting arbitrary keys on px.defaults; only documented keys are now accepted.
quote: px.defaults no longer accepts arbitrary keys [#2168]

subject: update_annotations, update_shapes, update_layout_images
new: no longer require the patch argument
truth: Stop passing the patch argument to update_annotations, update_shapes, and update_layout_images; it is no longer required.
quote: update_annotations, update_shapes and update_layout_images now no longer require the patch argument, as per the docstring [#2167]

from_fact: no longer require the patch argument

subject: plotly.express generated plots default height
new: no default height (inherits default height of regular figures)
truth: plotly.express figures no longer default to 600px height; they now inherit the default figure height. To restore old behavior, set px.defaults.height=600 or pass height=600 to px functions.
quote: plotly.express generated plots no longer have a default height of 600 pixels, instead they inherit the default height of regular figures

from_fact: no default height (inherits default height of regular figures)

subject: add_annotations/add_shapes/add_images methods
old: defaulted to adding objects in paper coordinates
new: no longer default to paper coordinates; plotly.js determines default reference frame based on context
truth: Stop relying on add_annotations/add_shapes/add_images defaulting to paper coordinates; the reference frame is now determined by plotly.js based on context.
quote: Update the `add_annotations`/`add_shapes`/`add_images` methods to no longer default to adding objects in paper coordinates. This allows plotly.js to determine the default reference frame based on context

from_fact: defaulted to adding objects in paper coordinates
no longer default to paper coordinates; plotly.js determines default reference frame based on context

subject: graph_objects.indicator.Gauge.steps property
new: steps
truth: Stop using the misspelled `stepss` property name on `graph_objects.indicator.Gauge`; use `steps` instead.
quote: The name of the steps property of `graph_objects.indicator.Gauge` has been renamed from `stepss` to `steps`

subject: plotly.plotly module
new: chart_studio.plotly
truth: Stop using plotly.plotly; use chart_studio.plotly instead.
quote: Update `numpy.percentile` syntax to stop using deprecated alias [[#5483](https://github.com/plotly/plotly.py/pull/5483)], with thanks to @Mr-Neutr0n for the contribution!

subject: plotly.api module
new: chart_studio.api
truth: Stop using plotly.api; use chart_studio.api instead.
quote: Update `numpy.percentile` syntax to stop using deprecated alias [[#5483](https://github.com/plotly/plotly.py/pull/5483)], with thanks to @Mr-Neutr0n for the contribution!

subject: plotly.dashboard_objs module
new: chart_studio.dashboard_objs
truth: Stop using plotly.dashboard_objs; use chart_studio.dashboard_objs instead.
quote: Update `numpy.percentile` syntax to stop using deprecated alias [[#5483](https://github.com/plotly/plotly.py/pull/5483)], with thanks to @Mr-Neutr0n for the contribution!

subject: plotly.grid_objs module
new: chart_studio.grid_objs
truth: Stop using plotly.grid_objs; use chart_studio.grid_objs instead.
quote: Update `numpy.percentile` syntax to stop using deprecated alias [[#5483](https://github.com/plotly/plotly.py/pull/5483)], with thanks to @Mr-Neutr0n for the contribution!

subject: plotly.presentation_objs module
new: chart_studio.presentation_objs
truth: Stop using plotly.presentation_objs; use chart_studio.presentation_objs instead.
quote: Update `numpy.percentile` syntax to stop using deprecated alias [[#5483](https://github.com/plotly/plotly.py/pull/5483)], with thanks to @Mr-Neutr0n for the contribution!

subject: plotly.widgets.GraphWidget class
new: plotly.graph_objects.FigureWidget
truth: Stop using plotly.widgets.GraphWidget; use plotly.graph_objects.FigureWidget instead.
quote: The legacy `plotly.widgets.GraphWidget` class for displaying online figures hosted by Chart Studio as ipywidgets has been removed. Please use the offline, and much more capable, `plotly.graph_objects.FigureWidget` class instead.

subject: fileopt argument to chart_studio.plotly.plot
truth: Stop using the fileopt argument when calling chart_studio.plotly.plot; in-place modifications to previously published figures are no longer supported and a figure will always overwrite a figure with the same name.
quote: The `fileopt` argument to `chart_studio.plotly.plot` has been removed, so in-place modifications to previously published figures are no longer supported, and a figure will always overwrite a figure with the same name.

subject: default theme
old: no default theme or different default
new: 'plotly' template is the default theme
truth: All figures now use the 'plotly' template as the default theme instead of no template or a different default.
quote: The 'plotly' template is used as the default theme across all figures.

from_fact: no default theme or different default

subject: bundled geographic shape files for create_choropleth
old: geographic shape files bundled in core plotly package
new: moved to optional plotly-geo package
truth: Stop using create_choropleth without installing the optional plotly-geo package, or install plotly-geo to get geographic shape files.
quote: the bundled geographic shape files used by the create_choropleth figure factory have been moved to a new optional plotly-geo distribution package

from_fact: geographic shape files bundled in core plotly package

subject: create_choropleth and create_gantt return type
new: return Figure objects
truth: create_choropleth and create_gantt now return Figure objects instead of dictionaries, so code expecting dict methods (e.g. .keys(), .items()) will break.
quote: the create_choropleth and create_gantt figure factories now always returns Figure objects, rather than dictionaries

subject: return value of add_trace methods
new: return a reference to the calling figure
truth: Figure add trace methods (.add_trace, .add_traces, .add_scatter, etc.) now return the figure instead of the trace, so code that captures the return value expecting a trace will break.
quote: Figure add trace methods (.add_trace, .add_traces, .add_scatter, etc.) now return a reference to the calling figure, rather than the newly created trace

from_fact: return a reference to the calling figure

subject: datetime objects with timezones
new: not converted to UTC
truth: datetime objects that include timezones are no longer converted to UTC, so timezone-aware datetimes will be displayed as-is instead of being normalized to UTC.
quote: datetime objects that include timezones are not longer converted to UTC

subject: tuple property update with longer list
new: extra elements appended to end of tuple
truth: When updating a tuple property (e.g. layout.annotations) with a longer list, extra elements are now appended instead of being truncated or causing an error.
quote: When a tuple property (e.g. layout.annotations) is updated with a list/tuple that is longer than the current value, the extra elements are appended to the end of the tuple.

from_fact: extra elements appended to end of tuple

subject: default colorscale for plotly, plotly_white, and plotly_dark templates
new: plasma
truth: The default colorscale for the plotly, plotly_white, and plotly_dark templates is now plasma instead of the previous default.
quote: Changed the default colorscale to be `plasma` for the `plotly`, `plotly_white`, and `plotly_dark` templates for plotly.py version 4

subject: default colorway order for plotly, plotly_white, and plotly_dark templates
new: reordered colorway
truth: The default colorway (sequence of colors for traces) for the plotly, plotly_white, and plotly_dark templates has been reordered.
quote: Reordered the default colorway for the `plotly`, `plotly_white`, and `plotly_dark` templates for plotly.py version 4

subject: layout.legend.itemsizing in built-in templates
new: reverted to previous value (not 'constant')
truth: The layout.legend.itemsizing setting in built-in templates was reverted from 'constant' back to the previous default, so legend entries may appear smaller than in 3.8.0.
quote: Reverted change to `layout.legend.itemsizing = 'constant'` in built-in templates that was made in 3.8.0. This resulted in unexpectedly large legend entries in some common cases

subject: title properties
new: title is a compound object; string assigned to title.text
truth: Stop setting layout.title as a plain string; set layout.title.text instead, or pass a string which will be mapped to title.text.
quote: Plotly.js 1.43 converted `title` properties (e.g. `layout.title`) from strings into compound objects that contain the text as the `text` property along with new title placement attributes `x`, `y`, `xref`, `yref`, `xanchor`, `yanchor` and `pad`. Plotly.py 3.5.0 follows the new schema, but still supports specifying `title` as a string, in which case the string is assigned to the `title.text` property

from_fact: title is a compound object; string assigned to title.text

subject: titlefont properties
new: layout.title.font, layout.xaxis.title.font, etc.
truth: Stop using titlefont, titlefontfamily, etc. as top-level properties; use title.font, title.font.family, etc. instead.
quote: Plotly.js 1.43 also moved existing `title*` properties (e.g. `layout.titlefont`) under the `title` object (e.g. `layout.title.font`). Plotly.py 3.5.0 follows the new schema, but still supports the legacy `title*` properties by mapping them to the corresponding `title.*` property

subject: update method return value
new: update() returns the updated graph object
truth: The update method on graph_objs now returns the updated object, enabling chaining; code that relied on update returning None may break if it checks the return value.
quote: The `update` method on `graph_objs` now returns the updated object in order to support chaining multiple update operations together

from_fact: update() returns the updated graph object

subject: show_link default
new: show_link defaults to False
truth: The 'send data to cloud' button is now hidden by default; set show_link=True explicitly if you need the link/button.
quote: The `show_link` option has been set to `False` by default in the offline `plot` and `iplot` functions.

subject: unknown config options
old: unknown config options were silently dropped
new: unknown config options produce a warning but are still passed to plotly.js
truth: Passing unknown config keys now triggers a warning instead of being silently ignored; code that relied on silent dropping may see warnings.
quote: `config` options that are not known by plotly.py result in a warning but are still passed along to plotly.js. Prior to this change these unknown options were dropped silently

from_fact: unknown config options were silently dropped
unknown config options produce a warning but are still passed to plotly.js

subject: Plotly logo in modebar button
old: old Plotly logo in 'Produced with Plotly' modebar button
new: new Plotly logo in 'Produced with Plotly' modebar button
truth: The 'Produced with Plotly' modebar button now uses the new Plotly logo instead of the old one.
quote: Use new Plotly logo in "Produced with Plotly" modebar button

from_fact: old plotly logo in 'produced with plotly' modebar button
new plotly logo in 'produced with plotly' modebar button

subject: area traces
new: barpolar trace type
truth: Stop using 'area' trace type; use 'barpolar' trace type instead, which replaces and augments area traces.
quote: Added `barpolar` trace type - which replace and augment area traces

subject: Figure/FigureWidget constructor argument
new: skip_invalid argument
truth: Stop using `_raise` argument in Figure/FigureWidget constructors; use `skip_invalid` instead.
quote: This argument replaces the `_raise` argument that was available in version 2

subject: Figure/FigureWidget method
new: to_ordered_dict method
truth: Stop using `get_ordered()` method on Figure/FigureWidget; use `to_ordered_dict()` instead.
quote: This method replaces the `get_ordered` method that was available in version 2

subject: data validation
old: invalid properties/values were silently ignored or accepted
new: invalid properties/values now always raise a ValueError with a description
truth: Passing invalid properties or values to graph objects now raises a ValueError instead of being silently accepted.
quote: Improved data validation covering the full API with clear, informative error messages. This means that incorrect properties and/or values now always raise a ValueError with a description of the error, the invalid property, and the available properties on the level that it was placed in the graph object. Eg. go.Scatter(foo=123) raises a validation error.

from_fact: invalid properties/values were silently ignored or accepted
invalid properties/values now always raise a valueerror with a description

subject: plotly.figure_factory.create_choropleth
truth: Stop using the 'offline_mode' parameter in create_choropleth; it has been removed from the call signature.
quote: 'offline_mode' param has been removed from call signature.

subject: plotly.figure_factory.create_choropleth
old: centroid points selection was not automatically enabled
new: persistent selection API for centroid points is automatically enabled
truth: Persistent selection API for centroid points is now automatically enabled in create_choropleth, which may change existing plot interactivity.
quote: Persistent selection api for the centroid points is automatically enabled.

from_fact: centroid points selection was not automatically enabled
persistent selection api for centroid points is automatically enabled

subject: plotly.figure_factory.create_choropleth
old: FIPS values on hover were not zero-padded
new: FIPS values on hover are 0-padded to 5 digits
truth: FIPS values displayed on hover are now 0-padded to ensure they are 5 digits, which may break code that parses or expects unpadded FIPS strings.
quote: FIPS values that appear on hover are 0-padded to ensure they are 5 digits.

from_fact: fips values on hover were not zero-padded
fips values on hover are 0-padded to 5 digits

subject: plotly.figure_factory.create_choropleth
old: hover_info default was not 'none' for county lines data
new: hover_info='none' is now default for county lines data
truth: The default hover_info for county lines data is now 'none', so county line hover info will no longer appear unless explicitly set.
quote: hover_info='none' is now default for the county lines data.

from_fact: hover_info default was not 'none' for county lines data
hover_info='none' is now default for county lines data

subject: figure_factory.create_gantt and figure_factory.create_dendrogram
old: returned something other than a Plotly figure
new: return a Plotly figure
truth: Stop relying on create_gantt and create_dendrogram returning non-Plotly-figure types; they now return a Plotly figure.
quote: `figure_factory.create_gantt` and `figure_factory.create_dendrogram` now return a Plotly figure (consistent with other figure factory chart types).

from_fact: returned something other than a plotly figure

subject: offline.init_notebook_mode()
new: optional when using offline.iplot()
truth: Stop calling offline.init_notebook_mode() before offline.iplot() if you were relying on it being mandatory; it is now optional.
quote: `offline.init_notebook_mode()` is now optional when using `offline.iplot()`.

subject: plotly.offline.plot and plotly.offline.iplot
new: now accept various configuration options
truth: plotly.offline.plot and plotly.offline.iplot now accept configuration options as arguments; existing code passing positional arguments may break if the new config options change argument order or expected types.
quote: `plotly.offline.plot` and `plotly.offline.iplot` now accept various [configuration options](https://plot.ly/javascript/configuration-options/) for their arguments.

subject: exception type for network failures
old: could be PlotlyError, PlotlyRequestException, or requests.exceptions.RequestException
new: always PlotlyRequestException
truth: Network failures now always raise plotly.exceptions.PlotlyRequestException instead of possibly PlotlyError or requests.exceptions.RequestException.
quote: `plotly.exceptions.PlotlyRequestException` is *always* raised for network failures. Previously either a `PlotlyError`, `PlotlyRequestException`, or a `requests.exceptions.ReqestException` could be raised.

from_fact: could be plotlyerror, plotlyrequestexception, or requests.exceptions.requestexception

subject: sign_in validation
old: sign_in did not validate against server
new: sign_in validates against server and raises PlotlyError on failure
truth: plotly.py:sign_in now validates to the plotly server specified in your config and raises a PlotlyError if it cannot make a successful request.
quote: `plotly.py:sign_in` now validates to the plotly server specified in your config. If it cannot make a successful request, it raises a `PlotlyError`.

from_fact: sign_in did not validate against server
sign_in validates against server and raises plotlyerror on failure

subject: figure_factory numpy dependency
new: figure_factory raises ImportError if numpy is missing
truth: plotly.figure_factory will raise an ImportError if numpy is not installed.
quote: `plotly.figure_factory` will raise an `ImportError` if `numpy` is not installed.

from_fact: figure_factory raises importerror if numpy is missing

subject: FigureFactory class
new: plotly.figure_factory.*
truth: Stop using plotly.tools.FigureFactory; use plotly.figure_factory.* instead.
quote: `plotly.tools.FigureFactory`. Use `plotly.figure_factory.*`.

subject: private imported flags
truth: plotly.tools._*_imported private flags (e.g. _numpy_imported) have been removed.
quote: `plotly.tools._*_imported` It was private anyhow, but now it's gone. (e.g., `_numpy_imported`)

subject: _api_v2 helper
truth: plotly.py._api_v2 has been removed.
quote: `plotly.py._api_v2` It was private anyhow, but now it's gone.

subject: plot-schema update on import
new: plot-schema is no longer updated on import
truth: The plot-schema from https://api.plot.ly/plot-schema is no longer updated on import.
quote: The plot-schema from `https://api.plot.ly/plot-schema` is no longer updated on import.

from_fact: plot-schema is no longer updated on import

subject: Grid column unique-identifiers format
old: old format of unique-identifiers in columns of grids uploaded via plotly.plotly.upload
new: new format of unique-identifiers
truth: Grid column unique-identifiers uploaded via plotly.plotly.upload now use a different format, which may break existing plots that reference grid columns by their old identifier.
quote: Fixed a bug in the format of unique-identifiers in columns of grids that are uploaded to plotly via `plotly.plotly.upload`. See https://github.com/plotly/plotly.py/pull/599 for details. In particular, creating plots that are based off of plotly grids is no longer broken.

from_fact: old format of unique-identifiers in columns of grids uploaded via plotly.plotly.upload

subject: create_distplot default histnorm
new: default is now 'probability density'
truth: create_distplot now defaults to 'probability density' instead of 'probability' for the histnorm parameter.
quote:
Changed the default option for `create_distplot` in the figure factory from `probability` to `probability density` and also added the `histnorm` parameter to allow the user to choose between the two options.
Note: This is a backwards incompatible change.

subject: create_scatterplotmatrix
new: colormap parameter
truth: Stop using `palette` in `create_scatterplotmatrix`; use `colormap` instead.
quote: The parameter `palette` has been replaced by `colormap`

subject: create_scatterplotmatrix
truth: Stop using `use_palette` in `create_scatterplotmatrix`; it has been removed.
quote: `use_palette` has been removed

subject: create_trisurf
new: color_func parameter
truth: Stop using `dist_func` in `create_trisurf`; use `color_func` instead.
quote: In `create_trisurf`, the parameter `dist_func` has been replaced by `color_func`

subject: init_notebook_mode
old: could call init_notebook_mode multiple times without consequence (1.9.13 behavior)
new: must call init_notebook_mode before iplot; it is now idempotent
truth: You must call `init_notebook_mode` before making an `iplot` call; calling it multiple times is safe but required.
quote: Version 1.10.0 brings back the requirement to call `init_notebook_mode` before making an `iplot` call.

from_fact: could call init_notebook_mode multiple times without consequence (1.9.13 behavior)
must call init_notebook_mode before iplot; it is now idempotent

subject: offline mode
old: Offline mode deleted Jupyter Notebook's require, requirejs, and define variables
new: Offline mode no longer deletes those variables
truth: Offline mode no longer deletes Jupyter Notebook's require, requirejs, and define variables, so code relying on those globals being absent will break.
quote: Offline mode will no longer delete the Jupyter Notebook's require, requirejs, and define variables.

from_fact: offline mode deleted jupyter notebook's require, requirejs, and define variables
offline mode no longer deletes those variables

subject: offline plotly usage
old: offline plotly required a paid license
new: offline plotly is now free and uses bundled plotly.js
truth: Offline plotly no longer requires a paid license; plotly.js is shipped inside the package for unlimited free use in IPython notebooks.
quote: Previously, using plotly offline required a paid license. No more: `plotly.js` is now shipped inside this package to allow unlimited free use of plotly inside the ipython notebook environment.

from_fact: offline plotly required a paid license
offline plotly is now free and uses bundled plotly.js

subject: plotly.offline.download_plotlyjs
old: download_plotlyjs function existed and was used to independently download plotly.js
truth: Stop using `plotly.offline.download_plotlyjs`; it is deprecated and plotly.js is no longer downloaded independently from this package.
quote: The `plotly.js` bundle that is required for offline use is no longer downloaded and installed independently from this package: `plotly.offline.download_plotlyjs` is **deprecated**.

from_fact: download_plotlyjs function existed and was used to independently download plotly.js

subject: streaming validation
truth: Stop relying on streaming validation; the 'is streamable' and streaming validation checks have been removed.
quote: No more *is streamable*, streaming validation.

subject: plotly.plotly.plot and plotly.plotly.iplot
new: sharing keyword argument with options 'public' | 'private' | 'secret'
truth: Stop using world_readable; use sharing='public'|'private'|'secret' instead.
quote: sharing keyword argument for plotly.plotly.plot and plotly.plotly.iplot with options 'public' | 'private' | 'secret' to control the privacy of the charts. Depreciates world_readable

associative: how to make a chart secret

subject: iplot
old: height and width parameters accepted directly in iplot()
truth: Stop passing height and width as arguments to iplot; set them in the figure's layout dict instead.
quote: height and width are no longer accepted in iplot. Just stick them into your figure's layout instead

from_fact: height and width parameters accepted directly in iplot()

subject: plot and iplot
old: silently ignoring error messages in the response from plot or iplot
new: raising an exception if the response contains an error message
truth: If the response from plot or iplot contains an error message, an exception is now raised instead of silently continuing.
quote: If the response from plot or iplot contains an error message, raise an exception

from_fact: silently ignoring error messages in the response from plot or iplot
raising an exception if the response contains an error message
